detail: Adopt file-backed state, anti-loop direction diversity, ready-means-execute, and proposer/evaluator separation for VLINCS no-anchor research.

Run a bounded DINO/fused-DINO edge-table target-repair challenger because direct DINO merging and small-island attachment are closed, but feature-source candidate retrieval may still expose repairable false-split targets.

Generated fused-DINO edge table with 3240 candidates and 47 eval-only true false-split edges; best no-oracle rule remained low precision. Single no-GT target repair selected 15 edges and 8 localized targets but full IDF1 was 0.652948, below standing 0.655240.

Even tiny target-repair grids spend minutes recomputing all-pairs pair metrics. The next implementation should cache edit signatures, use incremental pair deltas, or pre-rank repairability before full pair sweeps.

Added edit-signature caching and reran fused-DINO target repair. Best pair F1/P/R was 0.769755/0.815757/0.728664 with 27 selected edges and 23 localized targets, below the current model-side best 0.775234.
Implemented no-anchor NFC feature centralization. NFC time-agglom reached pair F1 0.594775, NFC softcut primary 0.764329, and NFC softcut auxiliary 0.767759; all are below the relevant baselines.
Close DINO target-repair expansion and NFC smoothing. Next branch should be a delivery-aware repairability proxy that predicts full-score side effects before identity edits are committed.

Patched foundation extractor for processor_model/get_image_features outputs, extracted MarketaJu/siglip2-person-description-reid features for all 9734 tracklets, and ran cheap gates. SigLIP2 alone reached pair F1 0.574380; 0.03/0.05 fusion component-merge runs were no-ops at 0.769661.

From the current conflict-reassign assignment, pure component merge accepted one edge but full IDF1 stayed 0.653541; micro split-then-merge split 41 conflict-heavy components, rewrote 2252 tracklets, and dropped pair F1 to 0.618505 and full IDF1 to 0.602445. Bulk split is too destructive; next branch should use surgical source-group edits with full-score side-effect gating.

Added --rank-by full_proxy to conflict_reassign. The no-GT proxy selected a conservative one-edit row with pair F1 0.772654 and full IDF1 0.653823, better than the pair-top4 row at 0.653541 but still below the standing e2e gate 0.655240. The proxy over-selects duplicate versions of the same top edit; next work needs diversity-aware source-group repair or a learned edit acceptor.

De-duplicated accepted-edit signatures before full scoring proxy-ranked surgical candidates. The 1/2/3-edit unique rows reached full IDF1 0.653823 / 0.653823 / 0.653042, all below the standing gate 0.655240. This confirms the current source-group repair family is saturated; next branch should be a learned edit acceptor or deliberately diverse source proposer.

Added --full-selection diverse_first_edge to sample full-score candidates by distinct first source-to-target edit. Local py_compile and self-test passed, but h100-test-3 could not be trusted for a run because Pluto service failed to connect and SSH timed out during banner exchange. Next retry should verify/restore the remote script, then run the diverse-first-edge full-proxy sweep.

Added --candidate-skip-first-edge-families to conflict_reassign so the proposer can skip the top N source->target edge families before greedy assignment construction. Added kit/run_no_anchor_false_split_diversity.sh to deploy and run skip values 0,1,2,4,8,16,32,64 with full_proxy/diverse_first_edge full scoring. Local py_compile, self-test, launcher bash -n, and dry-run path passed; remote execution remains blocked by Pluto banner-exchange timeout.

Added analyze_no_anchor_full_proxy_training.py, exported a compact ridge full-proxy model from 32 comparable non-oracle no-anchor proposal rows, and wired conflict_reassign --rank-by learned_proxy --learned-proxy-json. Clean audit LOOCV corr is 0.996050 with MAE 0.000913; this model is not promoted until a fresh DS1 full run beats the standing gate.

The cleaned compact learned proxy over-ranked state-policy rows at predicted full IDF1 0.7478 because their pair F1 was 0.9547, but the same policy had known full IDF1 0.0854 after delivering only 421-431 tracklets. This is now treated as a required negative, not an outlier to filter away.

Added no-GT delivery features and harvest hygiene to full-proxy training, excluded pair_candidates/training_audit artifacts, and added present-field delivery filtering to candidate scoring. The delivery-aware model predicts the dangerous low-coverage state-policy rows at 0.08535-0.08592 and ranks full-delivery conflict/louvain candidates at the top; top predicted candidate is 0.656057.

Local py_compile, JSON validation, solver self-test, and launcher bash -n passed. Dry-run probes for h100-test-3, h100-test-2, and test-video-0 all failed with SSH 'Connection timed out during banner exchange', so the delivery-aware diverse-first-edge full sweep has not been submitted yet.

Built an eval-only no-GT source-island acceptor from 61 audit rows / 12 positives. Baseline source_rank_score AP was 0.302423, while LOOCV ridge-logit AP reached 0.944124 and captured 9/10 positives in top-10. This is not a DS1 completion result, but it is a structural proposer upgrade for source-island candidate search.

Added --source-acceptor-json to conflict_reassign candidate search, scoring each source island with the exported no-GT ridge-logit acceptor and adding source_acceptor_score into proposal_score. Updated kit/run_no_anchor_false_split_diversity.sh to package the source acceptor plus delivery-aware full proxy.

After wiring the source acceptor, local py_compile, solver self-test, launcher bash -n, and JSON validation passed. A dry-run probe for h100-test-3 still failed before execution with SSH 'Connection timed out during banner exchange', so the source-acceptor delivery-proxy sweep has not been submitted.

Fresh Pluto checks for h100-test-3, h100-test-2, and test-video-0 failed at the API layer with 'Failed to connect to Pluto service'; direct h100-test-3 dry-run still failed at SSH banner exchange. Remote full scoring remains unavailable this turn, so progress shifted to local branch preparation and oracle-gap distillation.

The current softcut oracle repair decomposition shows base full IDF1 0.653071, oracle_all_gt_majority 0.706202, and split_top_40_then_merge_top_40 full IDF1 0.706008. This suggests the e2e>70 gap is mostly structured false-split/false-merge repair, not per-video detection filtering; the next no-GT branch should mimic top-k repair with edge budgets rather than threshold-only global merging.

Added --max-accepted-edges-grid and --one-edge-per-component to no_anchor_assignment_multiview_merge_sweep.py, preserving old behavior by default. Added kit/run_no_anchor_false_split_budget_merge.sh to run chained and diverse budgeted bridge sweeps with budgets 10/20/40/80 and full-top-12. Local py_compile, launcher bash -n, and synthetic budget/diversity checks passed; h100-test-3 dry-run remains blocked by SSH banner timeout.

Added --self-test to no_anchor_assignment_multiview_merge_sweep.py and fixed direct-script sys.path setup before vlincs_gallery import. The remote budgeted bridge launcher now runs py_compile and the self-test before launching chained/diverse full-score sweeps. Local validation target: py_compile, self-test, launcher bash -n, dry-run probe, and JSON state checks.

Local py_compile, solver --self-test, launcher bash -n, progress/directions/findings JSON validation all passed. A dry-run probe for h100-test-3 still failed before execution with SSH 'Connection timed out during banner exchange', so no new DS1 full score was produced.

Added rank-by committee_proxy to no_anchor_assignment_conflict_reassign_sweep.py. It keeps the delivery-aware learned full proxy as the main full-score ranking signal and adds a small source_acceptor_score bonus above a floor, so source-island repairability can break ties among similarly safe candidates. Also fixed direct-script sys.path before vlincs_gallery import. Local py_compile, solver --self-test, and launcher bash -n passed; h100-test-3 dry-run remains blocked by SSH banner timeout.

Added no_anchor_fullscore_scheduler.py to convert existing no-anchor candidate rows into a guarded full-score manifest. The scheduler filters anchor/GT-training rows, already-full-scored rows below the current best, low-delivery/low-pair rows, and coarse duplicate solver families. On delivery-proxy candidates it read 150 raw rows, kept 69 eligible, and selected 11 diverse unscored candidates. Result gate still has pass_joint=false; no new full score was produced because Pluto remains unavailable.

Distilled Deli AutoResearch into the VLINCS no-anchor loop: persist state to files, treat metric drops as honest evidence, force structural pivots after stale iterations, separate candidate generation from full-score evaluation, and prefer direction diversity over deeper sweeps in a saturated threshold family. This supports the next pivot from generic bridge thresholds to target-localized tiny-fragment repair.

Added a no-anchor tiny-fragment cannot-link override to no_anchor_assignment_multiview_merge_sweep.py. Default behavior is unchanged; when enabled, only high-scoring bridges whose smaller original component is below a swept size and whose large/small ratio clears a swept threshold can bypass cannot-link. Added the tiny_fragment_override branch to run_no_anchor_false_split_budget_merge.sh. Local py_compile, solver --self-test, and launcher bash -n passed; no new full score was produced yet.

After adding the tiny-fragment override branch, fresh Pluto status checks for h100-test-3 and h100-test-2 still failed with 'Failed to connect to Pluto service'. Dry-run SSH probes for both jobs failed during banner exchange, so the new branch was not submitted and no DS1 full-score artifact was produced.

Added analyze_no_anchor_target_fragment_rules.py, a CSV-only eval audit for no-anchor component edge tables. On the softcut edge table, small_side<=2 and score>=0.75 found 13/13 true top false-split edges with precision 1.0. On the DINO-fused edge table, small_side<=2 and max(source_rank,target_rank)<=3 found 17/17 true top false-split edges with precision 1.0. On the combined table, small_side<=2, max(source_rank,target_rank)<=3, and forbidden_only found 19/19 true top false-split edges with precision 1.0. This uses GT only for post-hoc analysis and motivated adding --edge-rank-maxes plus a tighter tiny_fragment_override launcher grid.

Extended no_anchor_edge_table_target_repair_sweep.py to read source_rank/target_rank as edge_rank_max, expose --max-edge-ranks, support direct-script sys.path setup, and add --self-test. Added run_no_anchor_edge_rank_target_fragment.sh to launch softcut and DINO-fused table-driven target-fragment sweeps with small_side<=2-style grids, edge-rank gates, require_forbidden, full-top-12, and assignment export. Local py_compile, solver self-tests, launcher bash -n passed. Pluto API status and SSH dry-run probes for h100-test-3 and h100-test-2 still failed, so no new DS1 full score was produced.

Distilled the open Deli AutoResearch/self-play thread into an executable no-anchor opponent guard. Added kit/no_anchor_deli_opponent.py: it reads the result gate and progress state, rejects pair-only winners when full DS1 IDF1/HOTA disagree, and forces a structural pivot when stale_count is high. On local_runs/no_anchor_result_gate_all_20260620.json it returned verdict=pivot: best global/pair candidate has pair F1 0.954691 but full IDF1 0.085412; best gated full IDF1 is 0.654009, below the standing 0.655240. This preserves the next action: submit edge-rank target-fragment and tiny-fragment sweeps when Pluto/SSH recovers.

After the Deli opponent guard selected the edge-rank target-fragment branch, fresh probes for h100-test-3, h100-test-2, and test-video-0 still failed. Pluto status returned 'Failed to connect to Pluto service' for all three; SSH dry-run probes timed out during banner exchange. No new DS1 full-score artifact was produced; this is an infrastructure pause, not an algorithmic refutation.

Fixed kit/no_anchor_result_gate.py so the evaluator reads JSON rows/full_rows/top_full_rows sections, maps idf1/hota/assa rows to full_idf1/full_hota/full_assa, and excludes oracle/GT-analysis/GT-filter-selection rows from production no-anchor eligibility. Rebuilt local_runs/no_anchor_result_gate_all_20260620.json: pass_joint remains false, best production e2e is correctly recovered as 0.655240 from no_anchor_softcut_then_softoverlap_density_filter_selector_zip_20260619.json, and best joint remains 0.654009. Rebuilt the Deli opponent verdict with best_gate_e2e_idf1=0.655240 and verdict=pivot.

Strict scheduling over 150 delivery-proxy candidate rows kept 12 eligible rows and selected 2 diverse candidates; exploratory scheduling keeps 69 eligible and 11 selected. No new DS1 full score was produced yet because Pluto/SSH is still unavailable.

Edge-rank target-fragment launcher dry-runs for both jobs passed local bash preflight but failed SSH probing with 'Connection timed out during banner exchange'. The strict full-score manifest and edge-rank/tiny-fragment sweeps remain prepared but unsubmitted.

A broad strict scheduler pass over 4349 pair/pair-only rows revealed that learned-proxy candidate rows could hide known-bad full-score evidence when they referenced an artifact but did not inline delivery/full fields. no_anchor_fullscore_scheduler.py now reads local artifact JSON/CSV rows, matches by mode and pair metrics, and imports known_full_idf1 plus delivery fields before eligibility. The assignment_state_policy false positive with pair F1 0.954690, full IDF1 0.085412, and only ~431 delivered tracklets is now rejected by known_below_current_best and low_delivery. Broad strict scheduling now keeps 26 eligible rows and selects 5 candidates; delivery strict remains 12 eligible / 2 selected.

After artifact-provenance guard validation, h100-test-3 and h100-test-2 Pluto status checks still failed with 'Failed to connect to Pluto service'. Edge-rank target-fragment launcher dry-runs for both jobs still fail during SSH banner exchange. No new DS1 full-score artifact was produced this loop.

The broad strict scheduler had one remaining duplicate-budget leak: learned-candidate rows with accepted_preview signatures and artifact rows with source_component_label/target_component could describe the same edit but receive different scheduler_family keys. no_anchor_fullscore_scheduler.py now prefers component-family keys, and when a candidate references a local artifact it reads the matching pair row to recover component labels before falling back to signature/source_target. Broad strict scheduling over 4349 rows remains 26 eligible but now selects 4 unique candidates instead of 5.

After scheduler family canonicalization, h100-test-3 and h100-test-2 Pluto status checks still failed with 'Failed to connect to Pluto service'. False-split diversity and edge-rank target-fragment launcher dry-runs still fail during SSH banner exchange. No new DS1 full-score artifact was produced this loop.

Added kit/analyze_no_anchor_edge_mass_budget.py to measure how much oracle false-split true-pair mass no-GT edge-table rules can cover. Combined softcut+DINO-fused audit: base full IDF1 0.653071, oracle full IDF1 0.706202, missing true-pair mass 9,105,079,781, and estimated coverage needed for IDF1 0.70 is 88.3% of the oracle gap. The best high-precision rule has 17/17 true edges, precision 1.0, but covers only 0.0448% of missing mass and estimates full IDF1 0.653095. A wide sweep's highest-coverage rule covers only 0.4329% and drops edge precision to 6.9%. Conclusion: target-fragment edge repair is a small safe fix, but the next research branch must target high-mass false-split component bridge/split selection with component-level side-effect modeling.

Patched no_anchor_assignment_conflict_reassign_sweep.py so candidate_search full scoring and assignment export restore the exact accepted_preview source_seqs->source_indices rather than rerunning ordinary _choose_targets. Added mass_bridge_proxy ranking, which explicitly rewards moved_tracklets and target_size while retaining visual target score, source_acceptor, margin, and side-effect proxy terms. Added kit/run_no_anchor_high_mass_bridge.sh to run a larger high-mass false-split bridge sweep with candidate_search_top_n=512, candidate_targets_per_source=2, diverse_first_edge full selection, and full_top_n=12. Local py_compile, solver --self-test, edge-mass --self-test, and launcher bash -n passed.

The high-mass launcher dry-run probe for h100-test-3 failed during SSH banner exchange. No new DS1 full-score artifact was produced.

Added --candidate-edge-rank-by proposal|mass_bridge to no_anchor_assignment_conflict_reassign_sweep.py. The new mass_bridge candidate-edge pre-ranker prevents the high-mass branch from truncating candidate_search_top_n by the old proposal_score before row-level mass_bridge_proxy can act. kit/run_no_anchor_high_mass_bridge.sh now sets --candidate-edge-rank-by mass_bridge. This is no-anchor/no-GT production logic; GT remains evaluation-only.

Added kit/analyze_no_anchor_oracle_gap_concentration.py and generated local_runs/no_anchor_oracle_gap_concentration_20260620.json plus reports/no_anchor_oracle_gap_concentration_20260620.md. On the current no-anchor oracle decomposition, top-1/3/5/10/20/30 false-split GT identities cover 11.5%/26.3%/39.2%/56.8%/80.9%/95.8% of missing true-pair mass. Top-20 false-merge components cover only 14.7% of base pred-pair mass. This supports focusing the next remote full-score budget on high-mass false-split component bridge/split selection.

Added top-level candidate_edge_rank_by provenance to no_anchor_assignment_conflict_reassign_sweep.py result JSON so high-mass artifacts record whether candidate_search_top_n was truncated by proposal or mass_bridge pre-ranking. Local py_compile, solver self-test, and high-mass launcher bash -n passed.

Pluto API status checks for h100-test-3, h100-test-2, and test-video-0 still return 'Failed to connect to Pluto service'.

Distilled the Deli AutoResearch/self-play lesson into the component-merge branch: preserve accepted-edge provenance and let the opponent spend full-score budget on high-mass false-split repairs. no_anchor_component_merge_sweep.py now emits accepted_preview plus score, rank-margin, weight, size-product, and mass-proxy aggregates. no_anchor_assignment_component_merge_sweep.py inherits the same fields and both paths support --rank-by mass_proxy. Updated no_anchor_fullscore_scheduler.py so assignment_component_merge rows with accepted_preview source/target or source_rep/target_rep are de-duplicated by actual component bridge family instead of falling back to artifact-level keys. Scheduler self-test now covers the new preview shape.

Added kit/analyze_no_anchor_edge_table_oracle_coverage.py and generated local_runs/no_anchor_edge_table_oracle_coverage_20260620.json/csv plus reports/no_anchor_edge_table_oracle_coverage_20260620.md. The softcut and DINO-fused edge acceptor tables together have 5240 candidate edges, 47 eval-only true edges, and 39,412,716 union true edge same-ID mass, covering only 0.00432865 of the 9,105,079,781 missing true-pair mass. Top-1 oracle GT coverage is 0.00030661 and top-3 coverage is 0.00013385; GT 36 and GT 11 have zero true edge coverage. Conclusion: edge-table repair is candidate-recall limited and should be treated as cleanup, not the main route to e2e 0.70.

no_anchor_assignment_multiview_merge_sweep.py now records accepted_preview and mass/score/view aggregates for accepted bridge edges, supports --rank-by mass_proxy, and its launcher run_no_anchor_false_split_budget_merge.sh now uses --rank-by mass_proxy with --accepted-preview-n 40. The goal is to spend full DS1 budget on high-mass false-split bridges instead of local pair-score winners. Production logic remains no-anchor/no-GT.

Refreshed analyze_no_anchor_full_proxy_training.py with accepted-edge provenance and mass features. local_runs/no_anchor_full_proxy_training_audit_mass_features_20260620.json reports 32 rows, 34 features, full-IDF1 range 0.602445..0.654009, and ridge LOOCV corr 0.969076 / MAE 0.001736 / RMSE 0.004556. Both prototype routes underperformed the S5 mean/std baseline: slot views reached identity/pair 0.442073/0.350859, all-cross views reached 0.443991/0.352414, versus mean/std 0.461553/0.360210. Eval-only retrieval audit also favored S5 mean OSNet over all-cross max: AP 0.430425 vs 0.372521 and cross positive margin 0.788167 vs 0.685156. Close this prototype aggregation route; next no-anchor branch should change identity evidence source or weak-label curriculum rather than aggregate the same OSNet crops harder.

Distilled the Deli AutoResearch/self-play thread into the current no-anchor loop as ready-means-execute: run concrete proxy candidates through canonical DS1 full-score instead of stopping at a queue. Then full-scored referee-pruned crossqueue/single-edge localized-island ranks 1-4 on h100-test-3. The proxy predicted full IDF1 0.662361-0.666790 with pair F1 around 0.769, but actual canonical IDF1/HOTA/AssA were rank1 0.650714/0.514125/0.529500, rank2 0.626798/0.494799/0.518994, rank3 0.624978/0.492621/0.516447, and rank4 0.625017/0.492936/0.517068, all below the current best 0.655911/0.519311/0.534922. The large 3->68 move causes severe DS1 side effects despite high pair F1. Close this old-base crossqueue component-reassignment family and pivot structurally to stronger no-anchor weak-label curriculum or new identity evidence with hard negatives from these failed moves.

Ran stricter no-anchor BoTSORT sample curriculum/top-k ablations after the crossqueue DS1 refutation. The key discovery was not the learned pair model but baseline time_agglom with top_k=15 and theta=0.035: sample identity F1 reached 0.702092 and pair F1/P/R reached 0.639093/0.607764/0.673829, correcting the prior S5 mean/std plateau 0.461553 identity and 0.360210 pair F1. Three curriculum variants all selected the same no-training baseline row, so the gain is a resolver search-space discovery. A direct DS1 migration probe with time_agglom top_k=15 theta 0.025-0.045 was negative: fast pair F1 peaked at 0.612019 with P/R 0.782720/0.502443, and canonical full IDF1/HOTA/AssA peaked at 0.587838/0.444591/0.463460, far below the current no-anchor best 0.655911/0.519311/0.534922. Promote the sample insight as a precision-biased candidate generator, but refute full-resolver replacement on DS1.

Applied the Deli AutoResearch execute-and-referee rule to the DS1 top_k15 time-agglom insight: use time_agglom only as a local candidate generator attached to the current no-anchor best, and score through the canonical assignment CSV -> full submission zip -> density_simple sourcezip -> p005 area path. A missed DATA_ROOT runner attempt produced empty HOTA input and was discarded. The four single attach candidates produced one micro-promotion and three hard negatives: rank08 moved seq 4973 from component 58 to 55 and improved IDF1/HOTA/AssA from 0.655911/0.519311/0.534922 to 0.655948/0.519356/0.534962; rank09/rank10/rank11 scored IDF1 0.655898/0.655843/0.655805. Promote rank08 as current best, close ungated combo spending for this family, and use rank08 vs rank09-11 as a tiny-attach side-effect critic dataset.

Extended the no-anchor top_k15 time-agglom attach generator with no-GT temporal/namespace features and a critic rank mode, then generated 21 relaxed candidates from the rank08 base. The hand critic was not a reliable ordering function: critic rank01 and rank03 scored IDF1 0.655939/0.655936, below rank08, while under-ranked critic rank04 promoted to 0.656068 and rank05 weakly promoted to 0.655963. Combining rank04+rank05 produced the new no-anchor e2e best 0.656083/0.519553/0.535174 IDF1/HOTA/AssA; combo 4+1 and 4+3 were lower at 0.656059/0.656057 IDF1. This adds verified positives and hard negatives for a learned/calibrated tiny attach ranker, but e2e remains far below 0.70.

Distilled the Deli AutoResearch/self-play protocol into the no-anchor loop, then trained a tiny ridge attach ranker from verified local-edit full-score labels. Candidate pools oldbase/rank08base/combo45base had 70/69/67 raw candidates. The ranker LOOCV was weak (corr 0.035787, MAE 0.000061451), but top selected edit 382+2821+7262->2329 moved 3 tracklets and promoted canonical p005-area IDF1/HOTA/AssA to 0.656141/0.519617/0.535233 from previous 0.656083/0.519553/0.535174. Ranks 2/3/4 scored p005 IDF1 0.655980/0.656133/0.656049. This confirms a small no-anchor scheduling signal but not enough to approach 0.70.

Three rolling no-anchor learned attach ranker iterations produced a verified micro-chain: combo 4+5 base 0.656083 -> roll1 382+2821+7262->2329 at 0.656141 -> roll2 9202->15 at 0.656193 -> roll3 6960->10 at 0.656225 IDF1. HOTA/AssA for the final best are 0.519723/0.535329. Roll3 improved MCAM06 Tc6 to IDF1 0.610296 and MCAM08 Tc6 to 0.769911, but MCAM04 Tc6 remains 0.562439. The marginal gains are real but too small for the 0.70 goal; stop blind ridge roll4 and build a side-effect/per-video gate or pivot to higher-mass evidence.

Built side-effect labels from 12 verified no-anchor local attach full-score edits and trained a tiny ridge side-effect gate. Diagnostics refuted the regressor as a production scheduler: global delta LOOCV corr was -0.481683, MCAM04 and MCAM06 were also anti-correlated, only MCAM08 was positive. Single-edit near-misses scored below the current best, and four side-effect combos also failed to beat roll3 rank04. The best corrected combo was 9487->33 + 2681->28 at p005 IDF1/HOTA/AssA 0.656200/0.519692/0.535294, still below the current 0.656225/0.519723/0.535329. A metric hygiene issue was caught and fixed: earlier rank02-04 p005 runs had an assignment path as config_name, so I wrote p005_area_config.txt on h100-test-3 and reran corrected p005 metrics. Conclusion: side-effect gate v1 is a useful label-producing refutation, not a promotion. Next should build richer per-video/undo side-effect labels or pivot to higher-mass component evidence, especially MCAM04.

Fed side-effect V1 single/combo outcomes back into the no-anchor side-effect label bank, increasing labels to 20, then trained a V2 MCAM04-weighted gate and scored the top four new candidates. V2 improved MCAM04 LOOCV corr to 0.260832 but global delta LOOCV remained anti-correlated at -0.256258. Canonical p005 scoring with the new guard produced no promotion: 6797->9 exactly tied the current best at IDF1/HOTA/AssA 0.656225/0.519723/0.535329, 1811+6864->46 scored 0.656182, 313->21 scored 0.656158, and 6234->17 scored 0.656214. This is a near-tie refutation of local attach-only side-effect iteration; pivot to high-mass component rollback/split and MCAM04 structural evidence.

Tested explicit no-anchor MCAM04 tracklet-level area admission on the current best roll3 rank04 assignment. The exporter generated six geometry-only policies without loading GT; the two mildest policies were canonical full-scored. mcam04_area2600 dropped 54 MCAM04 tracklets and scored p005 IDF1/HOTA/AssA 0.655613/0.519060/0.534957, while mcam04_area3000 dropped 91 and scored 0.655421/0.518834/0.534811, both below the standing 0.656225/0.519723/0.535329. MCAM04 DetPr rose and AssA rose slightly, but DetRe and IDF1 fell, so low-area tracklet pruning is the wrong lever. Stopped the more aggressive 3200-4800 sweeps and recorded this as a Deli-style honest negative ablation; pivot to recall-preserving structural/subpart conflict repair.

finding: Recall-guarded subpart repair promoted a new no-anchor e2e best without dropping tracklets. The relaxed rank01 edit moved six MCAM04 Tc6 tracklets from component 8 to 40 and improved canonical p005-area IDF1/HOTA/AssA from 0.656225/0.519723/0.535329 to 0.656453/0.519993/0.535718. The gain is small but structurally useful: MCAM04 IDF1 improved from 0.562439 to 0.563063 through DetRe 0.476722 -> 0.477831 while other focus videos stayed unchanged. Strict rank01 and relaxed ranks 2/4 were weaker positive/negative ablations. Continue with compatible subpart combinations and a side-effect label bank; e2e remains below 0.70.

Composed verified no-anchor subpart repairs from the current-best manifest and full-scored three canonical p005 combos. rank01+rank02 improved p005 IDF1/HOTA/AssA to 0.656548/0.520126/0.535863. rank01+rank04 was weaker at 0.656468/0.519934/0.535572, showing rank04 is association-risky. rank01+rank02+rank04 became the new IDF1 best at 0.656563/0.520067/0.535717, a +0.000110 IDF1 gain over the previous 0.656453. The result proves subpart repair is weakly compositional, but the gain remains far below the 0.70 e2e goal; next build a side-effect-aware subpart combo ranker/referee before spending on lower-margin rank05 candidates.

E2E remains below 0.70.

Full-scored 12 no-anchor multiview subpart combos on h100-test-3. No combo improved over the standing current best IDF1/HOTA/AssA 0.657653/0.520723/0.535819. Ten combinations tied exactly; the two combinations containing selection rank 3 (19->11) dropped to 0.657570, confirming that edit as a robust negative side-effect label. Blind composition of safe-neutral subpart edits does not amplify the 10->22 micro-promotion; next pivot to a trained/calibrated side-effect referee and new high-recall false-split repair sources.
